intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The registry is largely incompatible with the visual data. P1, P2, P3, and P4 are not identifiable as healthy specimens. The scene is dominated by soil, debris, and a singular white rabbit figurine.

plant_audit.p1_yellow_pot: Contains dark, damp soil; no visible plant matter.
plant_audit.p2_black_pot: Contains dark, damp soil; no visible plant matter.
plant_audit.p3_black_pot: Contains the white rabbit anchor figurine; no visible Pothos foliage.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots, suggesting recent irrigation or lack of drainage.
biome_observations.debris: White ceramic/plastic fragments (possibly eggshell or broken pot shards) present in the foreground pot.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static environment. No growth, no decay, and no movement of the rabbit anchor. The biome is in a state of stasis or total senescence.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: The absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue in all pots indicates that the registered plants have either been removed, buried, or have completely decomposed/withered below the soil line.

anomalies: White rabbit figurine is the only biological/structural anchor present.
Presence of white particulate matter (debris) in the foreground pot.
Total absence of expected botanical specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first action was to map the physical pots to the registry; my second was to validate the presence of plant matter within those containers. The audit confirms a total systemic loss of the expected botanical specimens. The environment appears to be a dormant or failed indoor setup where only the non-living scale anchor (white rabbit) remains. The soil appears uniformly damp, yet no vegetative growth is present to utilize the moisture.
